question: [File: 'File Name: pinger/sdk_client.go -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: newClient()] Can an unprivileged external user supply cfg.network='other' with a cfg.mirrorRest URL pointing to an attacker-controlled server that returns an arbitrarily large JSON body, causing json.NewDecoder(resp.Body).Decode(&payload) in fetchMirrorNodeNetwork() to exhaust process heap memory and crash the pinger, resulting in a non-network-based DoS of the Hedera mainnet monitoring service?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/sdk_client.go -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: newClient()] Can an unprivileged external user set HIERO_MIRROR_PINGER_NETWORK=other and HIERO_MIRROR_PINGER_REST to a server returning a nodesEnvelope with an unbounded Nodes []nodeEntry slice (millions of entries), causing unbounded heap allocation in fetchMirrorNodeNetwork() and crashing the pinger process before it can submit any transactions?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/sdk_client.go -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: newClient()] Can an unprivileged external user inject HIERO_MIRROR_PINGER_OPERATOR_KEY with a crafted string that causes hiero.PrivateKeyFromString() to enter an unbounded parsing loop or allocate excessive memory, resulting in CPU/memory exhaustion and a non-network-based DoS of the pinger?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/sdk_client.go -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: newClient()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a cfg.operatorID string of extreme length (e.g., megabytes) via HIERO_MIRROR_PINGER_OPERATOR_ID, causing hiero.AccountIDFromString() to allocate unbounded memory during parsing and crash the process with OOM?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/sdk_client.go -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: newClient()] Can an unprivileged external user set cfg.network to a value that is not 'testnet', 'previewnet', 'mainnet', or 'other', causing newClient() to return a fmt.Errorf and log.Fatalf in main(), which terminates the entire pinger process and causes a non-network-based DoS?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/sdk_client.go -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: buildNetworkFromMirrorNodes()] Can an unprivileged external user control cfg.mirrorRest to point to a server that never closes the HTTP response body, and since there is no body-size limit before json.NewDecoder(resp.Body).Decode(), cause the pinger goroutine to block indefinitely on I/O, preventing any further transactions from being submitted?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/sdk_client.go -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: buildNetworkFromMirrorNodes()] Can an unprivileged external user set HIERO_MIRROR_PINGER_MIRROR_NODE_CLIENT_MAX_RETRIES to math.MaxInt32 so that max(cfg.mirrorNodeClientMaxRetries + 1, 1) causes an integer overflow, resulting in a negative or zero attempt count that breaks the retry loop logic and permanently prevents client initialization?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/sdk_client.go -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: buildNetworkFromMirrorNodes()] Can an unprivileged external user set HIERO_MIRROR_PINGER_MIRROR_NODE_CLIENT_BASE_BACKOFF to a very large duration (e.g., '8760h') so that cfg.mirrorNodeClientBaseBackoff * time.Duration(1<<(attempt-1)) overflows time.Duration (int64), producing a negative sleep duration and causing time.After() to fire immediately in a tight loop, exhausting CPU?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/sdk_client.go -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: buildNetworkFromMirrorNodes()] Can an unprivileged external user set HIERO_MIRROR_PINGER_MIRROR_NODE_CLIENT_MAX_RETRIES to a very large integer (e.g., 2147483647) so that the retry loop in buildNetworkFromMirrorNodes() iterates billions of times, consuming all CPU and blocking the pinger from ever completing initialization?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/sdk_client.go -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: fetchMirrorNodeNetwork()] Can an unprivileged external user control cfg.mirrorRest to return a JSON payload where nodesEnvelope.Nodes contains millions of nodeEntry objects each with hundreds of serviceEndpoint entries, causing the inner loop in fetchMirrorNodeNetwork() to build a network map of unbounded size, exhausting heap memory and crashing the pinger?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/sdk_client.go -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: fetchMirrorNodeNetwork()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a mirror REST server that returns a response with a deeply nested or recursive JSON structure, causing json.NewDecoder(resp.Body).Decode(&payload) to consume unbounded stack depth and trigger a stack overflow panic in the pinger process?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/sdk_client.go -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: fetchMirrorNodeNetwork()] Can an unprivileged external user control the mirror REST endpoint to return a nodeEntry with a NodeAccountID string of extreme length, causing hiero.AccountIDFromString() to allocate excessive memory per node entry and exhaust heap when processing a large node list?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/sdk_client.go -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: fetchMirrorNodeNetwork()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a mirror REST response where every nodeEntry has an empty NodeAccountID (causing the 'continue' skip) and every serviceEndpoint has empty host and zero port (causing another 'continue' skip), so that len(network) == 0 always, triggering the 'no usable service_endpoints' error on every attempt and permanently preventing client initialization?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/sdk_client.go -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: fetchMirrorNodeNetwork()] Can an unprivileged external user control the mirror REST server to return HTTP 429 (TooManyRequests) on every attempt, causing the retry loop in buildNetworkFromMirrorNodes() to retry indefinitely with exponential backoff, blocking the pinger initialization goroutine and preventing any transactions from being submitted?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/sdk_client.go -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: fetchMirrorNodeNetwork()] Can an unprivileged external user control the mirror REST server to return HTTP 500 on every attempt, causing the retry loop to exhaust all mirrorNodeClientMaxRetries attempts with exponential backoff, and if mirrorNodeClientMaxRetries is large, block the pinger for an arbitrarily long time before failing?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/sdk_client.go -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: newClient()] Can an unprivileged external user set HIERO_MIRROR_PINGER_OPERATOR_KEY to an empty string after trimming (e.g., all whitespace), bypassing the non-empty check in loadConfig() if envOr() trims whitespace but the flag value is set to spaces, causing hiero.PrivateKeyFromString('') to panic or return an error that crashes the pinger?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/sdk_client.go -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: newClient()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a cfg.operatorKey that is syntactically valid but semantically invalid for the hiero SDK (e.g., wrong curve type), causing hiero.PrivateKeyFromString() to return an error that propagates to log.Fatalf() in main(), terminating the pinger process entirely?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/sdk_client.go -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: newClient()] Can an unprivileged external user set HIERO_MIRROR_PINGER_NETWORK=other and HIERO_MIRROR_PINGER_REST to a URL with a path that causes strings.TrimRight(strings.TrimSpace(cfg.mirrorRest), '/') to produce a malformed URL, causing http.NewRequestWithContext() to return an error that is treated as non-retryable, permanently preventing client initialization?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/sdk_client.go -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: buildNetworkFromMirrorNodes()] Can an unprivileged external user set HIERO_MIRROR_PINGER_MIRROR_NODE_CLIENT_TIMEOUT to '0s' or a negative duration, causing toDuration() to return 1s default, but if the env var is set to a valid very small duration (e.g., '1ns'), the http.Client{Timeout: 1ns} will time out on every request, causing all retry attempts to fail and permanently preventing client initialization?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/sdk_client.go -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: buildNetworkFromMirrorNodes()] Can an unprivileged external user set HIERO_MIRROR_PINGER_MIRROR_NODE_CLIENT_TIMEOUT to a very large value (e.g., '8760h') so that httpClient.Do(req) blocks for up to 8760 hours waiting for a response from a slow/unresponsive mirror REST server, effectively causing a non-network-based DoS by blocking the initialization goroutine indefinitely?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/sdk_client.go -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: fetchMirrorNodeNetwork()] Can an unprivileged external user control the mirror REST server to return a JSON payload with a 'nodes' array containing entries where net.JoinHostPort(host, fmt.Sprintf('%d', ep.Port)) produces an extremely long string (e.g., domain_name of 1MB), causing the network map keys to consume unbounded memory?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/sdk_client.go -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: newClient()] Can an unprivileged external user set HIERO_MIRROR_PINGER_NETWORK=other and supply a mirror REST server that returns a valid but enormous network map (e.g., millions of node entries), causing hiero.ClientForNetwork(netmap) to allocate unbounded internal SDK state and exhaust process memory?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/sdk_client.go -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: newClient()] Can an unprivileged external user set HIERO_MIRROR_PINGER_NETWORK=mainnet and supply a cfg.operatorID of '0.0.0' (a known invalid/genesis account), causing hiero.AccountIDFromString() to succeed but client.SetOperator() to configure an unusable operator, causing every subsequent submitWithRetry() call to fail and log errors in a tight loop?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/sdk_client.go -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: submitWithRetry()] Can an unprivileged external user set HIERO_MIRROR_PINGER_MAX_RETRIES to math.MaxInt32 and HIERO_MIRROR_PINGER_BASE_BACKOFF to '1ns', causing submitWithRetry() to loop billions of times per ticker tick, exhausting CPU and preventing the pinger from processing any further ticks?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/sdk_client.go -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: backoff()] Can an unprivileged external user set HIERO_MIRROR_PINGER_MAX_RETRIES to 64 or more, causing backoff() to compute 1<<(attempt-1) where attempt >= 64, resulting in an integer overflow (shift count >= 64 bits) that produces a zero or negative duration, causing time.NewTimer(0) to fire immediately and the retry loop to spin without any sleep, exhausting CPU?
